zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Edward Lewis ll於1500年出生在威爾士安格萊波迪德時，他的父親Hugh Ap Llewelyn31歲，母親Jane Buckeley29歲。他於1520年與Elizabeth Vaughn在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1595年享年95歲，埋葬在英國倫敦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Edward Lewis ll于1500年出生在威尔士安格萊波迪德时，他的父亲Hugh Ap Llewelyn31岁，母亲Jane Buckeley29岁。他于1520年与Elizabeth Vaughn在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1595年享年95岁，埋葬在英国倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Lewis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Lewis, Leweis, Lowis, from ancient Germanic (originally West Frankish) Hludwig (itself from hlōd- ‘fame, famous’ + wīg- ‘battle’). This was Latinized as Ludovicus and Chlodovisus, which were gallicized as Clovis or Clouis, French Louis. The name may also appear as Lawis, Laweys, Lawes, by unrounding of the vowel of Lowis on the analogy of the variation between Low and Law as pet forms of Middle English Lourence alias Laurence. This surname is also very common among African Americans. See Laws 2 and compare Lawrence .
Welsh: adopted for the Welsh personal name Llywelyn (see Llewellyn ).

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Lughaidh ‘son of Lughaidh’. This is one of the most common Old Irish personal names. It is derived from Lugh ‘brightness’, which was the name of a Celtic god.

variants.name: Laws
meanings: English: variant of Law (pet form of Lawrence ), with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Perhaps a shortened form of Scottish or Irish   McLeish  . Compare   McLaws  .

variants.name: Lewison
meanings: Welsh and English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name   Lewis   (in Wales this was taken as equivalent to the Welsh personal name   Llewelyn  ). In North America, this surname has reportedly absorbed in some cases the Greek patronymic   Iliopoulos  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): patronymic from the personal name Lewi, Polish-influenced form of Levy .

variants.name: McGlew
meanings: Irish: most probably from Mac Lughaidh ‘son of Lughaidh’, a Gaelic personal name (referencing the god Lugh), sometimes Anglicized as Lewis . However, both Woulfe and MacLysaght consider McGlew to be a shortened form of Mac Dhuinnshléibhe (see McAleavey and Dunleavy ).

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Edward Lewis ll was born in 1500, in Bodedern, Anglesey, Wales, his father, Hugh Ap Llewelyn, was 31 and his mother, Jane Buckeley, was 29. He married Elizabeth Vaughn in 1520, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1595, at the age of 95, and was buried in London, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Edward Lewis ll est né en 1500, à Bodedern, Anglesey, Galles, son père, Hugh Ap Llewelyn, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Jane Buckeley, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Vaughn en 1520, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1595, à l’âge de 95 ans, et a été enterré à Londres, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Lewis ll(이)가 1500년 웨일스 앤글레시 보데더에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh Ap Llewelyn(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane Buckeley(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1520년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Elizabeth Vaughn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1595년 95세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 런던에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Edward Lewis ll родился в 1500 г. в местности бодедирн, англеси, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Hugh Ap Llewelyn было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Jane Buckeley, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Vaughn в 1520 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1595 г. в возрасте 95 лет и был похоронен в местности Лондон, Англия.
